page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i1.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes 446 GB of bundled local SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing 8 physical cores with a single thread per core, supported by a 33 MB L3 cache.

In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits modest performance characteristics. Its stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics fall into the bottom 25% of tested servers, reflecting its older processor architecture. Memory read bandwidth is also relatively low, with small-block cached reads at 327.93 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 38.77 GB/sec. However, large-block memory write bandwidth performs at an average level of 35.61 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show lower-tier performance, with gzip compression recording 18.95 GB/sec and Passmark CPU encryption scoring 2720.69.

The inclusion of 446 GB of local SSD storage provides a resource-dense storage option without requiring external network-attached volumes, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from direct-attached storage. However, the trade-off is lower overall compute and memory performance compared to newer hardware generations. This instance is best suited for legacy general-purpose applications, basic web serving, lightweight database operations, and small-scale development or testing environments that require dedicated CPU cores and local SSD storage but do not demand high-throughput processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i1.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 446 GB of bundled local SSD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with stress-ng CPU and memory read bandwidth scores generally falling in the bottom 25% tier. However, the instance maintains average performance in large-block memory write bandwidth and small-model LLM inference. The primary advantage of this instance lies in its bundled local SSD storage, which offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-reliant workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, legacy web serving, and lightweight database operations that require dedicated cores and local storage without demanding high-performance compute.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring bundled local SSD storage and balanced memory configuration for legacy workloads.
